title: Poecilopteris lonchophora
taxonomy: Poecilopteris lonchophora belongs to the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. This plant is classified within the phylum Pteridophyta and class Polypodiopsida. As a member of the genus Poecilopteris, it is closely related to other species such as Poecilopteris recurvata and Poecilopteris subalata. The species Poecilopteris lonchophora is further classified into various species based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics. The classification of Poecilopteris lonchophora is essential for understanding its evolution, ecology, and distribution.

intro: Poecilopteris lonchophora, also known as the Asian polypody, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress in the body. The bioactive constituents of Poecilopteris lonchophora include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
